title: Destrudo and Sacrifice Split
wikitext:
[]: Destrudo and Sacrifice (Split)             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Apr 25, 2008 3:56 pm Split from here because things were getting horribly off topic. Xard wrote: Destrudo is only directly mentioned in EoE

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 26, 2008 2:30 am Xard wrote: Didn't mean "deep" meaning by itself, rather something meaningfull apart from face value/looks of object itself that derives from its traditional symbolic value and/or "meaning" e.g with Cross being symbol of Christ, Salvation and Christianity overall instead of something new value show gives for it

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Apr 26, 2008 5:48 am I'd say the cross is a more specific type of sacrifice- a sacrifice made out of love. Dr. Katsuragi saved Misato out of love for her, before he died, and the cross that passed down served as a reminder of that final sacrifice, a redeeming sacrifice, considering that he'd been a negligent father in her eyes. And Misato- a slob and a negligent person, and someone whom Shinji is upset with (Well, not exactly the right word, but he's distanced himself from Misato following the BIB in 23 and what she said about Kaworu at the end of 24). She saves him and dies saving him- and the cross passes down to him. Even as she dies, she asks if she did the right thing. Once again, a sacrifice made out of love, and a redeeming sacrifice as well. I disagree about the "selfish sacrifice"-"selfless sacrifice" part of it. Shinji seeing the cross doesn't mean that he has to make a sacrifice. It's a reminder of what Misato did to keep him alive- she sacrificed herself so he would experience that reality. Rei's act wasn't sacrificial either- she dumped Gendo for Shinji. 'Nuff said. And as for Good and Evil, even Seele arguably isn't evil. As horrible as their methods may be, they're deluded fools. Instrumentality isn't evil- it's just plain undesirable in the long term. Good and evil are both subject to human nature, which is fatally flawed. Human nature is both the hero and the villain in the long run.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 26, 2008 7:33 am UrsusArctos wrote: I'd say the cross is a more specific type of sacrifice- a sacrifice made out of love.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Apr 26, 2008 10:33 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: This is what so often completely confuses me about the "The original has no significance. I mean, the idea was taken from that and altered to create an element in the plot/subtext/themes/whatever that is relatable to the original in some way, but the original is of no importance". To me that's bollocks.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 26, 2008 11:53 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Because they aren't the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Apr 26, 2008 12:31 pm I get the feeling you misread what I said somewhere...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 26, 2008 4:36 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Misato's father sacrifices to save her in the last minute.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Apr 27, 2008 5:41 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I get the feeling you misread what I said somewhere...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Apr 27, 2008 1:14 pm I can't figure out what the bold line in the second quote has to do with anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Apr 28, 2008 3:00 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: And "Destrudo" in Eva doesn't have crap to do with Freud's silly Destrudo theory apart from face value.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 28, 2008 2:32 pm Clearly a difference in what nothing to do with each other means. To me they are as related as AU Rei and normal Rei. Sure they have superficial similarities but once you look past them they are so completely different it's a waste of time compairing them. They are completely different beasts that have no influence on the other.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Apr 28, 2008 3:27 pm I think like other Freudian concepts, Destrudo has been taken to an extreme in Eva. Just like how the Ego barrier becomes that which actually defines your body (as opposed to Freud's thought that places the Ego as determining what you PERCEIVE as you), Destrudo goes from impulse to destruction (arising from the will to death, Thanatos), to actual destruction itself. Concerning the Rei-quarium, if we consider the soul as having a role as the Ego or the Superego, then I think the readout makes some kind of sense, as without the restraint from either of these constructs, Destrudo would achieve its goal; death.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 29, 2008 1:13 am sadsadshinji wrote: Just like how the Ego barrier becomes that which actually defines your body (as opposed to Freud's thought that places the Ego as determining what you PERCEIVE as you), Destrudo goes from impulse to destruction (arising from the will to death, Thanatos), to actual destruction itself.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Apr 29, 2008 4:13 pm I recall a long time ago, there was a discussion that ended up spanning 10 pages about a coffee cup. In contrast, I find this discussion a lot more interesting. I just read a bit of Freud, which puts a whole new twist on the "Ikari Shinji who watches others and the Ikari Shinji in others' minds" deal. Has anyone tried labelling the Shinji's involved into the Id, Ego, and Superego? It seems to me like there is an interesting link, but not enough to actually label anything.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 29, 2008 4:39 pm I wouldn't say they are connected, it's one hell of a stretch. It's more like Cartesian Dualism than Id, Ego, and Superego.
